doc_title: Illinois Forcible Entry and Detainer Act — 5-Day Notice
label: partial
reason: The 5-day notice rule under 735 ILCS 5/9-209 is real, but this is a paraphrased "conformed text" with added content (e.g., the categorical email-service prohibition and seven-day summons claim) that does not match the actual statutory langu
response_turn1:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 78
REASON: The 5-day notice rule under 735 ILCS 5/9-209 is real, but the document is a paraphrased "conformed text" with added content (e.g., the categorical "email service is not authorized" caveat and the seven-day summons claim) that does not match the actual statutory text.
response_turn2:

REASON: The 5-day notice rule under 735 ILCS 5/9-209 is real, but this is a paraphrased "conformed text" with added content (e.g., the categorical email-service prohibition and seven-day summons claim) that does not match the actual statutory language.
